136: func isErrRetryLiveness(ctx context.Context, err error) bool {
137: 	if errors.HasType(err, (*kvpb.AmbiguousResultError)(nil)) {
138: 		// We generally want to retry ambiguous errors immediately, except if the
139: 		// ctx is canceled - in which case the ambiguous error is probably caused
140: 		// by the cancellation (and in any case it's pointless to retry with a
141: 		// canceled ctx).
142: 		return ctx.Err() == nil
143: 	} else if errors.HasType(err, (*kvpb.TransactionStatusError)(nil)) {
144: 		// 21.2 nodes can return a TransactionStatusError when they should have
145: 		// returned an AmbiguousResultError.
146: 		// TODO(andrei): Remove this in 22.2.
147: 		return true
148: 	} else if errors.Is(err, kv.OnePCNotAllowedError{}) {
149: 		return true
150: 	}
151: 	return false
152: }

177: 		Measurement: "Messages",

179: 	}

194: // Metrics holds metrics for use with node liveness activity.
195: type Metrics struct {
196: 	LiveNodes          *metric.Gauge
197: 	HeartbeatsInFlight *metric.Gauge

204: // IsLiveCallback is invoked when a node's IsLive state changes to true.
205: // Callbacks can be registered via NodeLiveness.RegisterCallback().
206: type IsLiveCallback func(livenesspb.Liveness)
207: 
208: // HeartbeatCallback is invoked whenever this node updates its own liveness status,
209: // indicating that it is alive.
210: // TODO(baptist): Remove this callback. The only usage of this is for logging an
211: // event at startup. This is a little heavyweight of a mechanism for that.
212: type HeartbeatCallback func(context.Context)
213: 
214: // NodeLiveness is a centralized failure detector that coordinates
215: // with the epoch-based range system to provide for leases of
216: // indefinite length (replacing frequent per-range lease renewals with
217: // heartbeats to the liveness system).
218: //
219: // It is also used as a general-purpose failure detector, but it is
220: // not ideal for this purpose. It is inefficient due to the use of
221: // replicated durable writes, and is not very sensitive (it primarily
222: // tests connectivity from the node to the liveness range; a node with
223: // a failing disk could still be considered live by this system).
224: //
225: // The persistent state of node liveness is stored in the KV layer,
226: // near the beginning of the keyspace. These are normal MVCC keys,
227: // written by CPut operations in 1PC transactions (the use of
228: // transactions and MVCC is regretted because it means that the
229: // liveness span depends on MVCC GC and can get overwhelmed if GC is
230: // not working. Transactions were used only to piggyback on the
231: // transaction commit trigger). The leaseholder of the liveness range
232: // gossips its contents whenever they change (only the changed
233: // portion); other nodes rarely read from this range directly.
234: //
235: // The use of conditional puts is crucial to maintain the guarantees
236: // needed by epoch-based leases. Both the Heartbeat and IncrementEpoch
237: // on this type require an expected value to be passed in; see
238: // comments on those methods for more.
239: //
240: // TODO(bdarnell): Also document interaction with draining and decommissioning.
241: type NodeLiveness struct {
242: 	ambientCtx        log.AmbientContext
243: 	stopper           *stop.Stopper
244: 	clock             *hlc.Clock
245: 	storage           Storage
246: 	livenessThreshold time.Duration
247: 	cache             *Cache
248: 	renewalDuration   time.Duration
249: 	selfSem           chan struct{}
250: 	otherSem          chan struct{}

149: // IsLiveMapEntry encapsulates data about current liveness for a
150: // node based on the liveness range.
151: // TODO(abaptist): This should only be used for epoch leases as it uses an
152: // overly strict version of liveness. Once epoch leases are removed, this will
153: // be also.
154: type IsLiveMapEntry struct {
155: 	Liveness
156: 	IsLive bool
157: }

159: // IsLiveMap is a type alias for a map from NodeID to IsLiveMapEntry.
160: type IsLiveMap map[roachpb.NodeID]IsLiveMapEntry

162: // NodeVitality should be used any place other than epoch leases where it is
163: // necessary to determine if a node is currently alive and what its health is.
164: // Aliveness and deadness are concepts that refer to our best guess of the
165: // health of this node. An alive node is one that should be treated as healthy
166: // for most decisions. A dead node is one that should be treated as unavailable
167: // for most decisions. A node can be neither dead or alive, if it has gone
168: // offline recently or if failing to consistently gossip. Different areas of the
169: // code have the flexibility to treat this condition differently.
170: type NodeVitality struct {
171: 	nodeID roachpb.NodeID
172: 	// draining is whether this node currently draining.
173: 	draining bool
174: 	// membership is whether the node is active or in a state of decommissioning.
175: 	membership MembershipStatus
176: 	// connected is whether we are currently directly connect to this node.
177: 	connected bool
178: 
179: 	// When the record is created. Records are not held for long, but they should
180: 	// always give consistent results when asked.
181: 	now                  hlc.Timestamp
182: 	timeUntilNodeDead    time.Duration
183: 	timeAfterNodeSuspect time.Duration
184: 
185: 	// Data that comes from the node/store descriptor cache.
186: 	descUpdateTime      hlc.Timestamp
187: 	descUnavailableTime hlc.Timestamp
188: 
189: 	// Data that comes from the liveness range
190: 	livenessExpiration hlc.Timestamp
191: 	livenessEpoch      int64
192: }
193: 
194: type NodeVitalityMap map[roachpb.NodeID]NodeVitality
195: 
196: type VitalityUsage int
197: 
198: const (
199: 	_ VitalityUsage = iota
200: 	IsAliveNotification
201: 	EpochLease
202: 	Rebalance
203: 	Admin
204: 	DistSQL
205: 	Upgrade
206: 	ConsistencyQueue
207: 	AdminHealthCheck
208: 	DecommissionCheck
209: 	SpanConfigConformance
210: 	ReplicaProgress
211: 	Metrics
212: 	LeaseCampaign
213: 	LeaseCampaignWeak
214: 	RangeQuiesience
215: 	NetworkMap
216: 	LossOfQuorum
217: 	ReplicaGCQueue
218: 	DistSender
219: )
220: 
221: func (nv NodeVitality) IsLive(usage VitalityUsage) bool {
222: 	switch usage {
223: 	case IsAliveNotification:
224: 		return nv.isAlive()
225: 	case EpochLease:
226: 		return nv.isAliveEpoch()
227: 	case Rebalance:
228: 		return nv.isAlive()
229: 	case DistSQL:
230: 		return nv.isAliveAndConnected()
231: 	case Upgrade:
232: 		return nv.isAlive()
233: 	case ConsistencyQueue:
234: 		return nv.isAvailableNotDraining()
235: 	case AdminHealthCheck:
236: 		return nv.isAvailableNotDraining()
237: 	case DecommissionCheck:
238: 		return nv.isAlive()
239: 	case SpanConfigConformance:
240: 		return nv.isAlive()
241: 	case ReplicaProgress:
242: 		return nv.isAliveAndConnected()
243: 	case Metrics:
244: 		return nv.isAlive()
245: 	case LeaseCampaign:
246: 		{
247: 			if !nv.isValid() {
248: 				// If we don't know about the leader in our liveness map, then we err on the side
249: 				// of caution and don't campaign, so assume it is alive.
250: 				return true
251: 			}
252: 			return nv.isAliveAndConnected()
253: 		}
254: 	case LeaseCampaignWeak:
255: 		{
256: 			if !nv.isValid() {
257: 				// If we don't know about the leader in our liveness map, then we err on the side
258: 				// of caution and don't campaign, so assume it is alive.
259: 				return true
260: 			}
261: 			return nv.now.Less(nv.livenessExpiration)
262: 		}
263: 	case RangeQuiesience:
264: 		{
265: 			if !nv.isValid() {
266: 				// If we don't know about the node, then we err on the side
267: 				// of caution and don't quiesce, so assume it is alive.
268: 				return true
269: 			}
270: 			return nv.isAliveAndConnected()
271: 		}
272: 	case NetworkMap:
273: 		return nv.connected
274: 	case LossOfQuorum:
275: 		return nv.isAlive()
276: 	case ReplicaGCQueue:
277: 		return nv.isAlive()
278: 	case DistSender:
279: 		return nv.isAvailableNotDraining()
280: 	}

6: package kvserver
7: 
8: import (
9: 	"context"
10: 
11: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvpb"
12: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/apply"
13: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/kvadmission"
14: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/kvserverbase"
15: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/kvserverpb"
16: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/raft"
17: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/roachpb"
18: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/storage/enginepb"
19: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/log"
20: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/protoutil"
21: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/timeutil"
22: 	"github.com/cockroachdb/errors"
23: 	"github.com/kr/pretty"
24: )
25: 
26: // replica_application_*.go files provide concrete implementations of
27: // the interfaces defined in the storage/apply package:
28: //
29: // replica_application_state_machine.go  ->  apply.StateMachine
30: // replica_application_decoder.go        ->  apply.Decoder
31: // replica_application_cmd.go            ->  apply.Command         (and variants)
32: // replica_application_cmd_buf.go        ->  apply.CommandIterator (and variants)
33: // replica_application_cmd_buf.go        ->  apply.CommandList     (and variants)
34: //
35: // These allow Replica to interface with the storage/apply package.
36: 
37: // applyCommittedEntriesStats returns stats about what happened during the
38: // application of a set of raft entries.
39: //
40: // TODO(ajwerner): add metrics to go with these stats.
41: type applyCommittedEntriesStats struct {
42: 	appBatchStats
43: 	followerStoreWriteBytes kvadmission.FollowerStoreWriteBytes
44: 	numBatchesProcessed     int // TODO(sep-raft-log): numBatches
45: 	stateAssertions         int
46: 	numConfChangeEntries    int
47: }
48: 
49: // replicaStateMachine implements the apply.StateMachine interface.
50: //
51: // The structure coordinates state transitions within the Replica state machine
52: // due to the application of replicated commands decoded from committed raft
53: // entries. Commands are applied to the state machine in a multi-stage process
54: // whereby individual commands are prepared for application relative to the
55: // current view of ReplicaState and staged in a replicaAppBatch, the batch is
56: // committed to the Replica's storage engine atomically, and finally the
57: // side-effects of each command is applied to the Replica's in-memory state.
58: type replicaStateMachine struct {
59: 	r *Replica
60: 	// batch is returned from NewBatch.
61: 	batch replicaAppBatch
62: 	// ephemeralBatch is returned from NewEphemeralBatch.
63: 	ephemeralBatch ephemeralReplicaAppBatch
64: 	// stats are updated during command application and reset by moveStats.
65: 	applyStats applyCommittedEntriesStats
66: 
67: 	// stats backs the currently open replicaAppBatch's view of the State field of
68: 	// kvserverpb.ReplicaState (which is otherwise backed directly by the
69: 	// Replica's in-memory state). This avoids an allocation per Stage() call as
70: 	// Stage() is now alloed to update this memory directly, whereas all other
71: 	// mutations need to copy-on-write.
72: 	stats enginepb.MVCCStats
73: }
74: 
75: // getStateMachine returns the Replica's apply.StateMachine. The Replica's
76: // raftMu is held for the entire lifetime of the replicaStateMachine.
77: func (r *Replica) getStateMachine() *replicaStateMachine {
78: 	sm := &r.raftMu.stateMachine
79: 	sm.r = r
80: 	return sm

25: func declareKeysRequestLease(
26: 	rs ImmutableRangeState,
27: 	_ *kvpb.Header,

31: 	_ time.Duration,

33: 	// NOTE: RequestLease is run on replicas that do not hold the lease, so
34: 	// acquiring latches would not help synchronize with other requests. As
35: 	// such, the request does not declare latches. See also
36: 	// concurrency.shouldIgnoreLatches().
37: 	latchSpans.DisableUndeclaredAccessAssertions()
38: 	return nil
39: }

41: // RequestLease sets the range lease for this range. The command fails
42: // only if the desired start timestamp collides with a previous lease.
43: // Otherwise, the start timestamp is wound back to right after the expiration
44: // of the previous lease (or zero). If this range replica is already the lease
45: // holder, the expiration will be extended or shortened as indicated. For a new
46: // lease, all duties required of the range lease holder are commenced, including
47: // releasing all latches and clearing the timestamp cache.
48: func RequestLease(
49: 	ctx context.Context, readWriter storage.ReadWriter, cArgs CommandArgs, resp kvpb.Response,
50: ) (result.Result, error) {
51: 	// When returning an error from this method, must always return a
52: 	// newFailedLeaseTrigger() to satisfy stats.
53: 	args := cArgs.Args.(*kvpb.RequestLeaseRequest)
54: 	prevLease := args.PrevLease
55: 	newLease := args.Lease
56: 
57: 	// If this check is removed at some point, the filtering of learners on the
58: 	// sending side would have to be removed as well.
59: 	// TODO(nvanbenschoten): move this into leases.Verify.
60: 	wasLastLeaseholder := prevLease.Replica.StoreID == newLease.Replica.StoreID
61: 	if err := roachpb.CheckCanReceiveLease(
62: 		newLease.Replica, cArgs.EvalCtx.Desc().Replicas(), wasLastLeaseholder,
63: 	); err != nil {
64: 		rErr := &kvpb.LeaseRejectedError{
65: 			Existing:  prevLease,
66: 			Requested: newLease,
67: 			Message:   err.Error(),
68: 		}
69: 		return newFailedLeaseTrigger(false /* isTransfer */), rErr
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	// Lease type switches need extra care to avoid expiration regressions.
73: 	if args.RevokePrevAndForwardExpiration {
74: 		// Stop using the current lease. All future calls to leaseStatus on this
75: 		// node with the current lease will now return a PROSCRIBED status. This
76: 		// stops the advancement of the expiration of the previous lease.
77: 		cArgs.EvalCtx.RevokeLease(ctx, prevLease.Sequence)
78: 
79: 		// After we revoke the previous lease, forward the new lease's minimum
80: 		// expiration beyond the maximum expiration reached by the now-revoked

35: 	// leaseholder. Additionally, it observes the state of the timestamp cache

37: 	//

39: 	// in the range, even though the only key the TransferLease actually writes

41: 	// request because every request must declare at least one addressable key.

45: 	// allow reads below the new lease timestamp through. However, doing so
46: 	// would only be safe if we also accounted for clock uncertainty in all read
47: 	// latches so that any read that may need to observe state on the new
48: 	// leaseholder gets blocked. We actually already do this for transactional
49: 	// reads (see DefaultDeclareIsolatedKeys), but not for non-transactional
50: 	// reads. We'd need to be careful here, so we should only pull on this if we

75: 	); err != nil {
76: 		return newFailedLeaseTrigger(true /* isTransfer */), err

Leader fortification is needed for a range to use a \"+\n31: \t\t\"Leader lease. Set to 0.0 to disable leader fortification and, by extension, \"+\n32: \t\t\"Leader leases. Set to 1.0 to enable leader fortification for all ranges and, \"+\n33: \t\t\"by extension, use Leader leases for all ranges which do not require \"+\n34: \t\t\"expiration-based leases. Set to a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to gradually \"+\n35: \t\t\"roll out Leader leases across the ranges in a cluster.\",\n36: \t// TODO(nvanbenschoten): make this a metamorphic constant once raft leader\n37: \t// fortification and leader leases are sufficiently stable.\n38: \tenvutil.EnvOrDefaultFloat64(\"COCKROACH_LEADER_FORTIFICATION_FRACTION_ENABLED\", 0.0),\n39: \tsettings.FloatInRange(0.0, 1.0),\n40: \tsettings.WithPublic,\n41: )\n42: \n43: // replicaRLockedStoreLiveness implements the raftstoreliveness.StoreLiveness\n44: // interface. The interface methods assume that Replica.mu is held in read mode\n45: // by their callers.\n46: type replicaRLockedStoreLiveness Replica\n47: \n48: var _ raftstoreliveness.StoreLiveness = (*replicaRLockedStoreLiveness)(nil)\n49: \n50: func (r *replicaRLockedStoreLiveness) getStoreIdent(\n51: \treplicaID raftpb.PeerID,\n52: ) (slpb.StoreIdent, bool) {\n53: \tr.mu.AssertRHeld()\n54: \tdesc, ok := r.shMu.state.Desc.GetReplicaDescriptorByID(roachpb.ReplicaID(replicaID))\n55: \tif !ok {\n56: \t\treturn slpb.StoreIdent{}, false\n57: \t}\n58: \treturn slpb.StoreIdent{NodeID: desc.NodeID, StoreID: desc.StoreID}, true\n59: }\n60: \n61: // SupportFor implements the raftstoreliveness.StoreLiveness interface.\n62: func (r *replicaRLockedStoreLiveness) SupportFor(replicaID raftpb.PeerID) (raftpb.Epoch, bool) {\n63: \tstoreID, ok := r.getStoreIdent(replicaID)\n64: \tif !ok {\n65: \t\tctx := r.AnnotateCtx(context.TODO())\n66: \t\tlog.Warningf(ctx, \"store not found for replica %d in SupportFor\", replicaID)\n67: \t\treturn 0, false\n68: \t}\n69: \tepoch, ok := r.store.storeLiveness.SupportFor(storeID)\n70: \treturn raftpb.Epoch(epoch), ok\n71: }\n72: \n73: // SupportFrom implements the raftstoreliveness.StoreLiveness interface.\n74: func (r *replicaRLockedStoreLiveness) SupportFrom(\n75: \treplicaID raftpb.PeerID,\n76: ) (raftpb.Epoch, hlc.Timestamp) {\n77: \tstoreID, ok := r.getStoreIdent(replicaID)\n78: \tif !ok {\n79: \t\tctx := r.AnnotateCtx(context.TODO())\n80: \t\tlog.Warningf(ctx, \"store not found for replica %d in SupportFrom\", replicaID)\n81: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cockroach--v24.3.0@2e3aaf185d0ed9361efdbf04cd7cd1153477d912/-/blob/pkg/kv/kvserver/replica_store_liveness.go%3FL1-80"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "1: // Copyright 2024 The Cockroach Authors.

23: 
24: // RaftLeaderFortificationFractionEnabled controls the fraction of ranges for
25: // which the raft leader fortification protocol is enabled.
26: var RaftLeaderFortificationFractionEnabled = settings.RegisterFloatSetting(
27: 	settings.SystemOnly,
28: 	"kv.raft.leader_fortification.fraction_enabled",
29: 	"controls the fraction of ranges for which the raft leader fortification "+
30: 		"protocol is enabled. Leader fortification is needed for a range to use a "+
31: 		"Leader lease. Set to 0.0 to disable leader fortification and, by extension, "+
32: 		"Leader leases. Set to 1.0 to enable leader fortification for all ranges and, "+
33: 		"by extension, use Leader leases for all ranges which do not require "+
34: 		"expiration-based leases. Set to a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to gradually "+
35: 		"roll out Leader leases across the ranges in a cluster.",
36: 	// TODO(nvanbenschoten): make this a metamorphic constant once raft leader
37: 	// fortification and leader leases are sufficiently stable.
38: 	envutil.EnvOrDefaultFloat64("COCKROACH_LEADER_FORTIFICATION_FRACTION_ENABLED", 0.0),
39: 	settings.FloatInRange(0.0, 1.0),
40: 	settings.WithPublic,
41: )

43: // replicaRLockedStoreLiveness implements the raftstoreliveness.StoreLiveness
44: // interface. The interface methods assume that Replica.mu is held in read mode
45: // by their callers.
46: type replicaRLockedStoreLiveness Replica
47: 
48: var _ raftstoreliveness.StoreLiveness = (*replicaRLockedStoreLiveness)(nil)
49: 
50: func (r *replicaRLockedStoreLiveness) getStoreIdent(
51: 	replicaID raftpb.PeerID,
52: ) (slpb.StoreIdent, bool) {
53: 	r.mu.AssertRHeld()
54: 	desc, ok := r.shMu.state.Desc.GetReplicaDescriptorByID(roachpb.ReplicaID(replicaID))
55: 	if !ok {
56: 		return slpb.StoreIdent{}, false
57: 	}
58: 	return slpb.StoreIdent{NodeID: desc.NodeID, StoreID: desc.StoreID}, true
59: }
60: 
61: // SupportFor implements the raftstoreliveness.StoreLiveness interface.
62: func (r *replicaRLockedStoreLiveness) SupportFor(replicaID raftpb.PeerID) (raftpb.Epoch, bool) {
63: 	storeID, ok := r.getStoreIdent(replicaID)
64: 	if !ok {
65: 		ctx := r.AnnotateCtx(context.TODO())
66: 		log.Warningf(ctx, "store not found for replica %d in SupportFor", replicaID)
67: 		return 0, false
68: 	}
69: 	epoch, ok := r.store.storeLiveness.SupportFor(storeID)
70: 	return raftpb.Epoch(epoch), ok
71: }

74: func (r *replicaRLockedStoreLiveness) SupportFrom(
75: 	replicaID raftpb.PeerID,
76: ) (raftpb.Epoch, hlc.Timestamp) {
77: 	storeID, ok := r.getStoreIdent(replicaID)
78: 	if !ok {
79: 		ctx := r.AnnotateCtx(context.TODO())
80: 		log.Warningf(ctx, "store not found for replica %d in SupportFrom", replicaID)

75: 		stopper:               stopper,
76: 		clock:                 clock,
77: 		sender:                sender,
78: 		receiveQueue:          newReceiveQueue(),
79: 		storesToAdd:           newStoresToAdd(),
80: 		requesterStateHandler: newRequesterStateHandler(),
